betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Pressurized Water Reactor (PWR)§f

§aStep up from the ZIRNOX — a modular 3D fission reactor that's safer than the RBMK!§f

The §bPWR§f is a modular fission reactor built in 3D. Unlike the RBMK, it does not iteratively calculate flux, meaning §eno runaway flux feedback loops§f occur. It is as modular as the RBMK but in 3D, making it safer, more stable, and gentler on meltdown.

§6Requirements§f:
§b• Fully enclosed casing§f (any shape) with a single Controller
§b• At least §e1 Fuel Rod§f and §e1 Neutron Source§f
§b• Control Rods§f between adjacent fuel rods to regulate reaction
§b• Heat Exchangers§f + §bCoolant Channels§f to transfer heat
§b• Access Ports§f for fluid I/O and fuel handling

§6PWR Component Blocks§f:
§b• Fuel Rod§f — holds PWR fuel, emits flux in 6 directions (15 block range)
§b• Control Rod§f — restricts neutron flux; all control rods operate in unison
§b• Heat Exchanger§f — transfers heat from core to coolant channels
§b• Coolant Channel§f — transfers heat to cooling fluid
§b• Heatsink§f — increases max core temp by §e5%§f each
§b• Neutron Source§f — provides constant flux to start the reaction
§b• Neutron Reflector§f — reflects neutrons back, reduces loss
§b• Pressure Vessel§f — casing block, blocks neutron radiation
§b• Access Port§f — fuel/fluid I/O
§b• Controller§f — single block per reactor, for assembly and GUI

§6Construction§f: All internal blocks must be fully enclosed by casing (any shape). One controller block only. Minimum 1 fuel rod + 1 neutron source. Right-click controller to assemble — issues appear in red.

§6Cooling Fluids§f (tagged [PWR Coolant]): §bCoolant§f, §bMUG Root Beer§f, §bHeavy Water§f, §bLiquid Sodium§f, §bLiquid Thorium Salt§f

§eSee PWR Operation & GUI quest for GUI controls, fuel management, and advanced operation!
